name: Noh Theater Mask

visual_attributes.morphology: oval-shaped face mask with exaggerated features
distinctive_features: small eye slits for limited visibility
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: approximately 20 cm in height and 15 cm in width
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: symmetrical facial features with a slight forward tilt

domain_specific_visual.character_representation: depicts roles such as ghosts, women, deities, and demons
domain_specific_visual.material: traditionally made from Japanese cypress wood

functional_attributes.primary_function: used in Noh theater performances to represent characters
secondary_functions: symbolizes the transformation of supernatural beings

domain_specific_functional.cultural_role: integral to the storytelling in Noh performances
domain_specific_functional.performance_impact: enhances the actor's portrayal of the character

relationships.description: Noh masks are essential components of Noh theater performances.
Crafted by skilled artisans specializing in traditional mask-making techniques.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents traditional Japanese performing arts and cultural heritage

contrastive_constraints: NOT a modern or abstract design
NOT associated with Western theatrical traditions
